sanskrit: निहत्य धार्तराष्ट्रान्नः का प्रीतिः स्याज्जनार्दन । पापमेवाश्रयेदस्मान्हत्वैतानाततायिनः ॥
hindiTranslation: हे जनार्दन इन धृतराष्ट्रसम्बन्धियों को मारकर हमलोगों को क्या प्रसन्नता होगी इन आततायियों को मारने से तो हमें पाप ही लगेगा ॥
englishTranslation: O Janardana, what joy would come to us by killing these sons of Dhritarashtra? Killing these aggressors would only bring us sin.
essence: Violence against kin brings no joy; it only deepens the wound.
speaker: Arjuna
simpleMeaning: Arjuna says that killing his own relatives will bring no happiness and will only stain the killers with wrongdoing.
simpleInsight: Moral clarity can feel painful, but it keeps you from confusing victory with peace.
detailedMeaning: On the Kurukshetra battlefield, Arjuna sees the war’s real cost: teachers, relatives, and family on both sides. Faced with the sons of Dhritarashtra, he says killing them would bring no joy and would burden the killers with wrongdoing.
modernRelevance: You are about to expose a lie that will break a family dinner open. The room goes silent, and you realize winning the argument would cost something deeper than being right.
translationLiteral: निहत्य (by killing) / धार्तराष्ट्रान् (the sons of Dhritarashtra) / नः (to us) / का (what) / प्रीतिः (joy) / स्यात् (would be) / जनार्दन (O Janardana) / पापम् (sin) / एव (only) / आश्रयेत् (would come upon) / अस्मान् (us) / हत्वा (having killed) / एतान् (these) / आततायिनः (aggressors)
